design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate platforming levels as a Toxic Crusader, defeat enemies using weapons, reach level exit
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Action-platformer combat navigation with enemy patterns and hazard avoidance
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Control a member of the Toxic Crusaders superhero team fighting toxins and mutants
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed platformer with weapon power progression system; weapon state resets between passwords (design choice limiting power carryover)

difficulty.lives_system: Standard NES platformer lives system (inferred from era and platform)
difficulty.continues_system: Password-based continuation system with four possible passwords per level
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based checkpointing via password system; no in-level checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited - debug functions allow invulnerability toggle and level skip, but not exposed in retail version

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon power level system (0-3 states); weapons reset to lowest level when using password continues
progression.unlock_mechanism: Linear level progression through six stages; hidden level select available via debug code (EESNEYAA Game Genie code)
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select enables non-linear stage access
Multiple password options per level suggest intended password sharing among players
Easter egg mechanics (red moon toggle in Level 2)

world_building_techniques: Cutscenes between levels (viewable via debug DEMO mode)
Dialogue and text boxes (MESSAGE mode in debug reveals all dialogue)
Licensed IP integration (Toxic Crusaders characters and setting)

danger_signals: Mutant enemies and hazards throughout levels
Red moon easter egg suggests environmental threat escalation
environmental_storytelling: Level design implies progression through toxic/mutant-infested environments
Unused 'mutant girl's head' graphics suggest thematic consistency

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Toxic Crusaders NES page)
